std_gy_rf_s_complementation_conjecture.note: Authored in X3.v:gyarfas_complementation_chi_bounded_statement. Encodes the Gyarfas complementation conjecture: complements of classes whose induced subgraphs satisfy chi<=omega+c are chi-bounded.

std_gy_rf_s_conjecture_6_8_h_h_h_1_class.note: Authored in X3.v:gyarfas_alpha_omega_chi_bounded_statement. Encodes Gyarfas Conjecture 6.8: the class satisfying alpha(H) omega(H) >= |V(H)|-1 for every induced subgraph H is chi-bounded.

std_gy_rf_s_s_rk_zy_monochromatic_compon.note: Authored in X20.v:gyarfas_sarkozy_monochromatic_component_statement. Encodes the Gyarfas-Sarkozy conjecture: for k>=3, minimum degree at least (1-(k-1)/k^2)n in a k-edge-coloured graph forces a monochromatic connected vertex set of order at least n/(k-1), with the degree and size inequalities cross-multiplied over naturals.

std_gy_rf_s_sumner_conjecture.note: Authored in X3.v:gyarfas_sumner_two_forbidden_statement. Encodes the two-forbidden-graphs Gyarfas-Sumner statement using absolute bounded chromatic number, complete graphs, and forests.

std_havel_s_problem.note: Authored in X69.v:havel_distant_triangles_three_colourable_statement. Encodes Havel's problem: planar graphs with pairwise distant triangles are 3-colourable.

std_jaeger_s_conjecture_high_girth_plana.note: Authored in X22.v:jaeger_high_girth_planar_odd_cycle_hom_statement. Encodes Jaeger's conjecture: every planar graph of girth at least 4k has a homomorphism to C_(2k+1).

std_kelly_manvel_conjecture_reconstructi.note: Authored in X21.v:kelly_manvel_n_minus_r_deck_reconstruction_statement. Encodes the Kelly-Manvel row: for every r there is N such that every n-vertex graph with N<=n is reconstructible from its (n-r)-deck.

std_kotzig_s_perfect_1_factorization_con.note: Authored in X25.v:kotzig_perfect_one_factorization_statement. Encodes Kotzig's conjecture: K_n has a perfect 1-factorization for every even n>2.

std_kowalik_lu_ar_krekovski_conjecture_i.note: Authored in X6.v:girth_five_planar_induced_forest_seven_tenths_statement. Encodes the planar girth-at-least-five induced-forest lower bound 7|V(G)|/10.

std_lichiardopol_s_conjecture.note: Authored in X19.v:lichiardopol_distinct_length_dicycle_packing_statement. Encodes Lichiardopol's conjecture: for every k there is a threshold g(k) so every nonempty loopless digraph with minimum outdegree at least g(k) contains k vertex-disjoint directed cycles of pairwise distinct lengths.

std_lov_sz_conjecture_on_r_partite_hyper.note: Authored in X6.v:lovasz_r_partite_matching_deletion_statement. Encodes the Lovasz r-partite r-uniform hypergraph matching deletion conjecture.

std_lov_sz_s_even_directed_cycle_conject.note: Authored in X44.v:lovasz_even_directed_cycle_statement. Encodes Lovasz's even directed cycle conjecture: some p makes every p-strongly connected digraph contain an even directed cycle.

std_n_dl_s_conjecture_deck_reconstructio.note: Authored in X21.v:nydl_tree_l_deck_reconstruction_statement. Encodes the Nydl tree deck row: for n>=4 and ell>=floor(n/2)+1, two n-vertex trees with the same ell-deck are isomorphic.

std_normal_graph_conjecture_de_simone_k_.note: Authored in X29.v:no_c5_c7_complement_c7_normal_graph_statement. Encodes the normal graph conjecture: no induced C5, C7, or complement of C7 implies normality.

std_planar_linear_arboricity_conjecture.note: Authored in X23.v:planar_linear_arboricity_statement. Encodes the planar linear arboricity conjecture: maximum degree Delta>=5 implies la(G)=ceil(Delta/2).

std_scott_seymour_tournament_chi_bounded.note: Authored in X70.v:scott_seymour_tournament_outneighbourhood_chi_statement. Encodes the Scott-Seymour tournament out-neighbourhood chi-boundedness conjecture.

std_small_quasi_kernel_conjecture_erd_s_.note: Authored in X16.v:small_quasi_kernel_statement. Encodes the Erdős-Székely small quasi-kernel conjecture: every loopless digraph with no source has a 2-kernel K with 2|K| <= |G|.

std_smith_s_conjecture_longest_cycles_in.note: Authored in X10.v:smith_longest_cycles_r_connected_statement. Encodes Smith's conjecture: for every r>=2, any two longest cycles in an r-connected graph meet in at least r vertices.

std_spinoza_west_connectedness_threshold.note: Authored in X21.v:spinoza_west_l_deck_connectedness_statement. Encodes the Spinoza-West connectedness threshold row: for n>=6 and ell>=floor(n/2)+1, same ell-deck implies connectedness is equivalent.

std_spiro_s_quasi_kernel_conjecture.note: Authored in X16.v:spiro_quasi_kernel_half_covered_statement. Encodes Spiro's conjecture: every loopless digraph has a 2-kernel K such that at least half the vertices lie in K or have an in-neighbour in K.

std_stein_s_conjecture_on_oriented_paths.note: Authored in X17.v:stein_oriented_paths_strict_semidegree_statement. Encodes Stein's conjecture: every nonempty oriented graph with strict minimum semidegree greater than k/2 contains every orientation of the k-edge path.

std_sumner_s_conjecture.note: Authored in X17.v:sumner_oriented_tree_unavoidable_statement. Encodes Sumner's conjecture: every oriented tree of order n>1 is (2n-2)-unavoidable in tournaments.

std_thomason_s_conjecture_on_book_ramsey.note: Authored in X4.v:thomason_book_ramsey_bound_statement. Encodes the diagonal book Ramsey bound r(B_n^(k)) <= 2^k(n+k-2)+2 using the standard k-spine, n-page book graph.

std_woodall_s_list_colouring_conjecture_.note: Authored in X12.v:woodall_ks_t_minor_free_list_colouring_statement. Encodes Woodall's list-colouring conjecture: for all positive s,t, every K_{s,t}-minor-free graph has choice number at most s+t-1.
